acceptance_targets: [chapter_03_award_001] PASS if the revised chapter contains at least one school-life scene in the back half where Gavin's manuscript obsession causes an immediate on-page consequence another person can register, and the final beat at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:175 lands on a changed relationship/state rather than only future setup. FAIL if the back half still reads as incremental puzzle progress plus a bridge into Chapter 4.

[chapter_03_craft_001] PASS if the revised late section includes at least one contiguous scene where the reader watches Gavin move directly from nighttime work into next-day impairment and sees that impairment alter his behavior or another character's response on page. FAIL if those costs remain primarily summarized in explanatory narration.

[chapter_03_prose_001] PASS if, after revision, the chapter uses the exact 'the way ...' construction no more than twice and the cited passages no longer present as same-shaped analogy sentences in succession. FAIL if three or more of the cited sites still rely on the same formula.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised chapter contains at least one school-life scene in the back half where Gavin's manuscript obsession causes an immediate on-page consequence another person can register, and the final beat at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:175 lands on a changed relationship/state rather than only future setup. FAIL if the back half still reads as incremental puzzle progress plus a bridge into Chapter 4.
findings.evidence: The chapter's second half turns Gavin's obsession into a sequence of neat clue beats and a sequel-hook close rather than an irreversible chapter-level collision between manuscript pressure and school life: snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:79, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:91, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:131, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:145, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:175.
findings.problem: The discovery material is strong, but the chapter does not reach shortlist-level consequence. Once Gavin has the manuscript, the narrative parcels the obsession into orderly mini-breakthroughs and then exits on setup for Chapter 4. That keeps the material interesting, but it isolates the uncanny from the occupied-school world instead of forcing them to contaminate each other in a way that feels irreversible inside this chapter.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the second-half hinge around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:97, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:145, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:175 so one ordinary Hogwarts scene is actively destabilized by the manuscript obsession on page. The revision should make Gavin's fixation alter a relationship, class outcome, or risk calculation in scene, then close on that changed state rather than only on the functional handoff that he will show someone a page.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised late section includes at least one contiguous scene where the reader watches Gavin move directly from nighttime work into next-day impairment and sees that impairment alter his behavior or another character's response on page. FAIL if those costs remain primarily summarized in explanatory narration.
findings.evidence: Once the sleeplessness starts costing him, the prose repeatedly steps back into recap: snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:163, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:169, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:171, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:173.
findings.problem: The late obsession stretch drifts into summary at the exact point the chapter should stay present inside Gavin's deterioration. Missed breakfast, botched schoolwork, social absence, and compulsive return to the sheets are mostly reported after the fact, which creates timidity/summary drift where the premise calls for more lived bodily and social consequence.
findings.rewrite_direction: Expand one cause-and-effect sequence across snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:157, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:163, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:171 into a fully dramatized scene. Keep Gavin in body and under pressure: show the sleepless morning, the shabby attempt to pass as normal, and either the classroom failure or the social failure unfolding in real time instead of compressing it into retrospective summary.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, after revision, the chapter uses the exact 'the way ...' construction no more than twice and the cited passages no longer present as same-shaped analogy sentences in succession. FAIL if three or more of the cited sites still rely on the same formula.
findings.evidence: The chapter leans on the same 'the way ...' explanatory comparison more than a dozen times, including snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:21, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:27, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:33, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:37, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:39, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:49, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:53, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:63, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:129, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:161.
findings.problem: A sentence shape that should feel selective becomes a prose tic. The repeated 'the way ...' template turns description and explanation into the same rhythm over and over, which flattens the uncanny texture and violates the brief's ban on recurring formulas after the second occurrence.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the repeated comparison sites at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:21, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:27, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:33, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:37, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:39, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:49, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:53, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:63, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:129, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:161. Keep only the few comparisons that do indispensable work, and replace the rest with direct sensory statement or materially different syntax so the chapter no longer leans on one descriptive formula.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel's central risk is tonal: it must sustain dark comedy, cosmic horror, and genuine emotional tenderness in the same narrative without any register canceling the others. Lean into the discomfort of juxtaposition. A scene where Gavin is mentally modeling ward-substrate decay while a classmate screams under the Cruciatus Curse is not a failure of empathy — it is the novel's core tension made visceral. The humor of the premise — that Voldemort's war is an irritating distraction from the real problem — must never be defused by making Gavin feel appropriately guilty at the right moments. He should feel guilty sometimes and not other times, and the reader should sit with the discomfort of a protagonist whose priorities have become genuinely alien. The cosmic material should be rendered with enough specificity that it is genuinely unsettling, not just philosophically interesting. Do not soften the ending: Gavin's refusal to intervene in the Battle of Hogwarts must be played straight, without a last-minute redemptive gesture or moral safety net.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism in this novel is a particularly acute failure because the entire thematic engine runs on the difference between seeing something directly and not seeing it. Gavin's journey is toward unmediated perception. The prose must mirror that commitment. When the Carrows torture a student, render the torture. When Gavin is hit with the Cruciatus, render the pain in his body. When the cosmic threat presses against perception, render what it feels like — do not retreat to abstractions about 'something vast' or 'an incomprehensible presence.' Find the specific sensory detail that makes the incomprehensible land. Euphemism in a novel about the failure to see what is real is thematic self-sabotage.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all dialogue. Uncontracted forms are reserved for deliberate emphasis, Babbling's academic register, or moments where a character is being pointedly formal. If a line reads stiffly, check contractions first.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate chapter textures aggressively. A high-intensity chapter (Cruciatus detention, Duncan's torture) must be followed by a chapter with a different pressure profile — quieter, more internal, more comic, or more focused on intellectual work. No two consecutive chapters should share the same dominant emotional register, the same scene-structure (e.g., two chapters that each build to a confrontation), or the same pacing profile. Vary scene count, dialogue density, and the ratio of interiority to action. Some chapters should be mostly one extended scene; others should move across multiple locations and timeframes. The Christmas chapter and the work-routine chapters should breathe differently from the Carrow chapters and the battle chapter.
prose_style_profile.diction: Plain, concrete, specific. Anglo-Saxon roots preferred over Latinate where both are available. Avoid poetic diction, literary allusion, and metaphors that call attention to their own cleverness. Metaphors should arise from Gavin's actual frame of reference: schoolwork, the castle, ordinary physical experience. When the First Art requires description, reach for precision over beauty — describe what it looks like, not what it means. The novel's most striking language should come from the collision between ordinary diction and extraordinary perception.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, locked to Gavin's perception throughout. The narrative voice has access to his thoughts, feelings, and sensory experience but not to anyone else's interiority. Other characters are known only through what Gavin observes. As the First Art restructures Gavin's cognition, the narrative's perceptual range should gradually expand — he begins noticing things no one else can see — but the voice remains grounded in his ordinary, slightly bewildered perspective. The narration should never become more articulate or literary than Gavin himself would be; the strangeness of what he perceives should press against the ordinariness of how he describes it.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied and responsive to scene pressure. Default rhythm is medium-paced prose with sentences of mixed length — some short, some compound, none habitually long. During scenes of intellectual discovery, let sentences extend as Gavin's attention extends, then cut short when comprehension fails. During Carrow scenes, prose should tighten and become more physical. During quiet scenes, allow room for observation and sensory detail without rushing toward the next event. Do not develop a signature rhythm that persists across all chapters — the rhythm should be the scene's rhythm, not the author's.
